title: Runtime kernel: AgentRunner, Pi adapter, Docker workspaces, and ToolExecutor
description:
## Summary
Create the adapter and workspace seam for coding agents: AgentRunner behaviour, AgentProfile capabilities, normalized agent event envelope, Pi RPC adapter, deterministic fake runner, Docker workspace materialization, patch capture, ToolExecutor boundary, cancellation, heartbeat, timeout, and adapter conformance.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Child tasks preserve the full feature and trust scope named in this epic; do not replace them with a narrower shortcut.
2. The Phase 0/1 verification matrix maps this epic and each child task to unit, integration, e2e or canary coverage plus required diagnostic logs/artifacts, or an explicit approved N/A.
3. Completion requires the relevant child tasks to satisfy their own acceptance criteria, dependency order, quality gates, and preserved run/evidence artifacts.

## Success Criteria
1. Relevant child tasks are complete with their own acceptance criteria, dependency order, quality gates, and evidence artifacts satisfied.
2. The Phase 0/1 verification matrix maps this epic and its child tasks to required unit, integration, e2e or canary coverage plus diagnostic logs/artifacts.
3. No feature, trust primitive, or user-visible workflow named in this epic is removed, narrowed, or silently deferred without an explicit human-approved contract change bead.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Rationale: Pi is first, not forever. The conductor state machine must remain stable when Codex, Claude Code, OpenHands, Aider, Goose, or another agent is swapped in. Capability snapshots determine autonomy ceiling, and raw agent output is never treated as proof.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Plan-space audit revision: this top-level epic is a planning container, not an implementation pick. Use its child tasks and dependency edges as the authoritative executable graph; the epic priority is intentionally lower so br/bv triage surfaces concrete work first.

title: Define AgentRunner behaviour and AgentProfile capability model
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Define Conveyor.AgentRunner callbacks, AgentProfile resources, and explicit capability snapshots for streaming events, pre-exec command policy, cancellation, diff capture, cost reporting, MCP support, slash commands, structured output, session resume, and known limitations.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. capabilities map deterministically to autonomy ceiling; negative capabilities are recorded in RunSpec; adapters can run below theoretical ceiling when host policy or credential posture is weaker.
2. Verification & logging: unit tests assert capabilities map deterministically to an autonomy ceiling, negative capabilities are recorded in RunSpec, and an adapter can run below its theoretical ceiling under weaker host policy, emitting a structured capability snapshot; shared with eop.9 conformance and mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: the adapter contract must be broader than Pi so future Codex/Claude/OpenHands/Aider/Goose adapters do not force a conductor rewrite.

title: Support observe-only adapter profile with lower autonomy ceiling
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Implement pi_in_container_observe_only or equivalent mode for adapters that cannot provide pre-exec command interception, with hardened sandbox constraints and explicit autonomy cap.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. RunSpec records observe_only policy capability; L1 local implementation may run when project policy allows; L2+ is rejected without pre-exec policy and credential broker integration.
2. Verification & logging: tests assert RunSpec records the observe_only capability, L1 local runs only when policy allows, and L2+ is rejected without pre-exec policy + credential broker, emitting a structured autonomy-cap decision log; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: capability gaps should reduce authority, not block experimentation entirely.

title: Normalize adapter events into the Conveyor agent event envelope
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Implement event normalization for session_started, message_delta, message_completed, command_requested, command_policy_decision, command_started, command_completed, file_change_observed, heartbeat, final_response, cancel_requested, cancel_acknowledged, adapter_error, and session_completed.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. events carry run_spec_sha256, run_attempt_id, agent_session_id, adapter, adapter session id, monotonic sequence number, event_version, raw_ref, and trace context.
2. Verification & logging: unit tests assert every normalized event (session_started..session_completed) carries run_spec_sha256/run_attempt_id/agent_session_id/adapter/seq/event_version/raw_ref/trace-context and a monotonic sequence, emitting a structured normalized-event log; shared with eop.9 and mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: LiveView, replay, policy, RunCheck, and conformance tests consume normalized events, not arbitrary raw transcripts.

title: Implement deterministic fake implementer and reviewer runners
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Build deterministic fake/patch implementer and deterministic reviewer fixture runner for default tests, demo, and CI.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. fake runner can produce known-good patch, labeled bad patches, malformed output, timeout, cancellation, and no-diff scenarios; no provider credentials are required; emitted events match the normalized envelope.
2. Verification & logging: tests assert the fake runner produces known-good patch, labeled bad patches, malformed output, timeout, cancellation, and no-diff scenarios with no credentials and envelope-matching events, emitting a structured scenario-replay log; underpins the vmr.14 harness and mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: default CI must prove the conductor loop without live model variability.

title: Implement SandboxRunner Docker materialize/exec/destroy adapter
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Implement SandboxRunner callbacks to materialize a repo at base commit, execute normalized commands under policy, and destroy/preserve workspaces according to cleanup policy.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. implement, gate, baseline, acceptance_calibration, canary, and post_integration purposes are supported; container id/path/head tree digest/cleanup state are recorded; failed workspaces are preserved only when policy allows.
2. Verification & logging: tests assert all purposes (implement, gate, baseline, acceptance_calibration, canary, post_integration) materialize/exec/destroy with container id/path/head-tree-digest/cleanup-state recorded and failed workspaces preserved only when policy allows, emitting a structured materialize/exec/destroy log; shared with vmr.11 and mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: Docker is an adapter behind SandboxRunner, not the conductor API.

title: Implement Pi RPC adapter over a BEAM Port
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Implement Conveyor.AgentRunner.Pi launching Pi in RPC/JSON mode through a BEAM Port, using pinned image/toolchain profile, scoped credentials, mounted workspace/cache roots, event streaming, heartbeat, budget limits, and final result capture.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. live Pi path works behind tagged/manual test; adapter errors and protocol churn are contained behind AgentRunner; final diff is captured by conductor rather than trusted from agent summary.
2. Verification & logging: a tagged/manual live conformance test plus a default-CI fake-protocol unit test assert adapter errors/protocol churn stay behind AgentRunner and the final diff is captured by the conductor (not trusted from the agent summary), emitting a structured port-protocol + event log; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix with the live path covered by vmr.10.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: Pi is first because its RPC seam is structured. Keep the live path optional until it earns default CI trust.

title: Route adapter tool calls through ToolExecutor where capabilities permit
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Connect adapters that support pre-exec interception to Conveyor ToolExecutor and Policy.Engine for command approval and execution inside the sandbox.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. command_requested events receive command_policy_decision events; blocked commands do not execute; observe-only profiles are clearly marked and autonomy-capped; all command attempts produce ToolInvocation records.
2. Verification & logging: tests assert command_requested events receive command_policy_decision events, blocked commands do not execute, observe-only profiles are marked/autonomy-capped, and every attempt yields a ToolInvocation, emitting a structured per-command decision log; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: MCP and slash-command transports are allowed only as routes to conductor-mediated tools, not policy bypasses.

title: Capture PatchSet from fresh base and apply it to clean gate workspace
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. After implementation, capture git diff as PatchSet with base commit, patch ref/digest, file lists, line counts, locked path touch flag, applies_cleanly, and generated timestamp.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. patch applies cleanly to a fresh gate WorkspaceMaterialization at base commit; head tree digest is recorded; dirty workspace state is not treated as evidence.
2. Verification & logging: tests assert the captured patch applies cleanly to a fresh gate workspace at base commit, head tree digest is recorded, and dirty workspace state is never treated as evidence, emitting a structured PatchSet summary + apply log; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: the patch artifact is the handoff between stochastic generation and deterministic verification.

title: Implement cancellation, heartbeat, idle timeout, and output limits
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Enforce max runtime, idle time, output size, sandbox quotas, cancellation behavior, and heartbeat gaps for agent sessions and station runs.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. cancel_requested/cancel_acknowledged/adapter_error/session_completed events are emitted as appropriate; exceeded limits stop the run according to RunBudget policy; stale sessions are eligible for reconciliation.
2. Verification & logging: tests assert max-runtime/idle/output/quota/heartbeat-gap limits emit the right cancel/adapter_error/session_completed events and stop per RunBudget policy with stale sessions eligible for reconciliation, emitting a structured limit-enforcement log; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: best-effort cancellation is acceptable for L1, but it must be recorded as a capability limitation.

title: Create AgentRunner conformance tests
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add conformance tests for capability reporting, startup, normalized event streaming, monotonic sequence numbers, cancellation, timeout behavior, diff capture, malformed output handling, policy semantics, and cost reporting behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. deterministic fake adapter passes by default; Pi live conformance is tagged/manual; failures identify which capability or event contract regressed.
2. Verification & logging: the conformance suite covers capability reporting, startup, normalized streaming, monotonic seq, cancellation, timeout, diff capture, malformed-output handling, policy semantics, and cost reporting with the fake adapter passing by default and Pi live tagged/manual, emitting a structured per-capability conformance report naming any regressed contract; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: adapter quality should be measurable before new agents are added to the matrix.

title: Trust kernel: evidence, schemas, artifacts, redaction, and replay
description:
## Summary
Create the evidence product surface: content-addressed artifacts, local .conveyor/runs projection, RunBundle manifest, JSON schemas, RunCheck validation, redaction/quarantine semantics, PR-body draft, dossier, provenance shape, R0 timeline replay, and R1 artifact regeneration.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Child tasks preserve the full feature and trust scope named in this epic; do not replace them with a narrower shortcut.
2. The Phase 0/1 verification matrix maps this epic and each child task to unit, integration, e2e or canary coverage plus required diagnostic logs/artifacts, or an explicit approved N/A.
3. Completion requires the relevant child tasks to satisfy their own acceptance criteria, dependency order, quality gates, and preserved run/evidence artifacts.

## Success Criteria
1. Relevant child tasks are complete with their own acceptance criteria, dependency order, quality gates, and evidence artifacts satisfied.
2. The Phase 0/1 verification matrix maps this epic and its child tasks to required unit, integration, e2e or canary coverage plus diagnostic logs/artifacts.
3. No feature, trust primitive, or user-visible workflow named in this epic is removed, narrowed, or silently deferred without an explicit human-approved contract change bead.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Rationale: evidence packets are not debug logs. They are the product artifact that lets a human trust, review, attach, and later automate work. Postgres remains source of truth; disk is a verified projection. The bundle root digest becomes Conveyor's first trust anchor.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Plan-space audit revision: this top-level epic is a planning container, not an implementation pick. Use its child tasks and dependency edges as the authoritative executable graph; the epic priority is intentionally lower so br/bv triage surfaces concrete work first.

title: Implement content-addressed artifact blob storage and local projection backend
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Build Conveyor.Artifacts.Projector behaviour and local-disk backend with .conveyor/blobs/sha256 and .conveyor/runs/<run_attempt_id> projection.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. bytes are stored by sha256 before projection; projection paths are not identity; digest verification happens before writing human-friendly files; backend seam leaves object storage deferred.
2. Verification & logging: unit tests assert bytes are stored by sha256 before projection, projection paths are not identity, and digests are verified before writing human files, emitting a structured blob/projection ledger + digest-mismatch finding; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: disk is a projection for humans and PRs. Postgres plus content-addressed blobs remain truth.

title: Implement retention policy and artifact tombstone events
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add RetentionPolicy behavior for public, internal, sensitive, redacted, and quarantined artifacts, plus ledger tombstone events for any allowed blob deletion.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. garbage collection never deletes a blob referenced by DB records unless policy allows it; deletion records artifact id, prior digest, reason, and actor.
2. Verification & logging: tests assert GC never deletes a DB-referenced blob unless policy allows and deletions record artifact id/prior digest/reason/actor as a ledger tombstone, emitting a structured retention/tombstone log; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: artifact retention is boring until secrets appear. Then it becomes trust-critical.

title: Implement JSON schemas and examples for evidence artifacts
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Provide JSON schemas and golden examples for manifest, evidence, review, gate, run bundle, provenance, and key result artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. every public artifact includes schema_version; valid examples pass; invalid examples fail with explicit finding categories; schema versions are recorded in RunSpec and reports.
2. Verification & logging: tests assert every public artifact carries schema_version, valid examples pass, and invalid examples fail with explicit finding categories, emitting a structured schema-validation report; shared with vmr.6 and mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.
close_reason: Implemented manifest@1, provenance@1, key_result@1 schemas/examples; recorded artifact schema versions in RunSpec/run bundle; validate_schema_registry reports 52/52 pass and UBS has 0 critical/warnings.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: artifact consumers should not need database access, but they must be able to trust schema and digest metadata.

title: Implement RunCheck schema and internal consistency validation
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. RunCheck validates artifact schemas, required refs, digest consistency, manifest entries, AC evidence mapping, review dossier digest, gate fields, command refs, sensitivity metadata, and run_spec_sha256 consistency.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. missing refs, mismatched hashes, invalid enums, absent AC evidence, unsupported schema versions, and stale canary refs fail deterministically.
2. Verification & logging: unit tests assert missing refs, mismatched hashes, invalid enums, absent AC evidence, unsupported schema versions, and stale canary refs each fail deterministically with a named category, emitting a structured RunCheck finding set; shared with vmr.6 and mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: RunCheck is the gate's immune system against artifact tampering and creative parsing.

title: Implement security redaction, quarantine, and export semantics
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Scan prompts, tool outputs, command logs, diffs, environment metadata, dossiers, and PR bodies for secrets.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. raw sensitive artifacts are classified sensitive/quarantined and not projected; redacted projections have their own digests; manifests distinguish raw_sha256 and redacted_sha256; blocked secret findings prevent gate success unless policy explicitly allows redacted continuation.
2. Verification & logging: fixtures with fake secrets in prompts/tool-output/logs/diffs/dossiers assert raw artifacts are quarantined (not projected), redacted projections get their own digests, and blocked secret findings prevent gate success unless policy allows, emitting separate raw/redacted digests + a redaction report; shared with vmr.5 and mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: never compare redacted bytes to raw output digests without knowing which digest is authoritative.

title: Implement machine evidence schema and EvidenceRecorder outputs
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. EvidenceRecorder writes evidence.json with run, Slice, agent, base/head, autonomy, summary, changed files, diff ref, commands re-run by conductor, AC statuses/evidence, quality refs, policy violations, review result, gate result, and known risks.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. evidence is created from independent conductor verification, not agent self-report; AC mapping is complete; output is digest-recorded and schema-valid.
2. Verification & logging: unit tests assert evidence is built from independent conductor verification (not agent self-report), AC mapping is complete, and output is schema-valid + digest-recorded, emitting a structured evidence summary + incomplete-mapping finding; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: agent narration is input, not evidence. Evidence starts after the conductor reproduces the claims.

title: Generate human dossier and PR-body draft from recorded evidence
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Generate dossier.md and pr_body.md with Slice, requirement traceability, summary, diff, AC-to-evidence mapping, conductor commands, quality delta, reviewer verdict, gate result, policy/safety, known risks, and bundle digests.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. dossier is useful without LiveView; PR body states task, summary, AC checkboxes, verification, risk, agent/profile, and evidence digests.
2. Verification & logging: tests assert the dossier is useful without LiveView and the PR body states task/summary/AC-checkboxes/verification/risk/agent-profile/evidence-digests, emitting a structured generation summary + missing-section finding; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: forcing a PR-quality body in Phase 1 keeps evidence quality aligned with the future L2 promise.

title: Emit standards-shaped provenance and optional SBOM artifacts
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Write unsigned local provenance.intoto.json shaped like in-toto/SLSA and optionally sbom.cyclonedx.json when toolchain data is available.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. provenance names diff, evidence, base commit, container image digest, test pack digest, run_spec_sha256, policy_sha256, and prompt_sha256; unsigned status is explicit.
2. Verification & logging: tests assert provenance names diff/evidence/base-commit/image-digest/testpack-digest/run_spec_sha256/policy_sha256/prompt_sha256 and unsigned status is explicit, emitting a structured provenance summary; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: SLSA/SBOM depth can grow later, but Phase 1 needs the shape and digest discipline now.

title: Implement canonical RunBundle manifest and root digest
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Create RunBundle manifest with canonical entries for evidence, dossier, review, gate, provenance, PR body, diff, logs, quality outputs, and canary results.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. bundle_root_sha256 excludes generated timestamps, host paths, and nondeterministic ordering; manifest identifies excluded fields; regeneration preserves root digest for identical records.
2. Verification & logging: tests assert bundle_root_sha256 excludes timestamps/host-paths/nondeterministic ordering and regeneration preserves the root for identical records, emitting the canonical manifest + excluded-field list + digest; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: the RunBundle root digest is Conveyor's first local trust anchor.

title: Implement R0 timeline replay and R1 artifact regeneration
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Implement replay levels for Phase 1: R0 rebuilds human timeline from LedgerEvent; R1 regenerates .conveyor/runs artifacts from DB records and content-addressed blobs.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. replay command can rebuild timeline and projection; regenerated files verify digests; missing/quarantined artifacts produce explicit findings rather than partial silent success.
2. Verification & logging: tests assert replay rebuilds the timeline (R0) and regenerates projections (R1) with verified digests, and missing/quarantined artifacts produce explicit findings (not silent partial success), emitting a structured replay report + gap findings; exercised via replay in the vmr.14 harness and mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: Phase 1 replay is not full event-sourced state reconstruction. Do not overbuild R2 yet.

title: Trust kernel: deterministic gate, reviewer-on-dossier, and canary harness
description:
## Summary
Implement the slice-level deterministic gate and the honesty checks around it: workspace integrity, diff scope, observed risk, policy, secret safety, tests, acceptance mapping, contract lock validation, quality delta, RunCheck, provenance, reviewer aggregation, reviewer health, and gate canary freshness.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Child tasks preserve the full feature and trust scope named in this epic; do not replace them with a narrower shortcut.
2. The Phase 0/1 verification matrix maps this epic and each child task to unit, integration, e2e or canary coverage plus required diagnostic logs/artifacts, or an explicit approved N/A.
3. Completion requires the relevant child tasks to satisfy their own acceptance criteria, dependency order, quality gates, and preserved run/evidence artifacts.

## Success Criteria
1. Relevant child tasks are complete with their own acceptance criteria, dependency order, quality gates, and evidence artifacts satisfied.
2. The Phase 0/1 verification matrix maps this epic and its child tasks to required unit, integration, e2e or canary coverage plus diagnostic logs/artifacts.
3. No feature, trust primitive, or user-visible workflow named in this epic is removed, narrowed, or silently deferred without an explicit human-approved contract change bead.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Rationale: the gate is the human stand-in. A passed mutant is a Conveyor bug, not a successful run. This epic proves the gate can fail labeled bad patches and only use reviewer judgment when the reviewer is schema-valid, actor-separated, and calibrated.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Plan-space audit revision: this top-level epic is a planning container, not an implementation pick. Use its child tasks and dependency edges as the authoritative executable graph; the epic priority is intentionally lower so br/bv triage surfaces concrete work first.

title: Implement deterministic Gate stage engine and result composition
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Build Gate stage composition that evaluates required stages, records per-stage details, produces GateResult, and transitions attempts/Slices only when all required stages pass.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. any required failure blocks; failure findings name stage and next action; gate result includes gate version, gate code digest, policy digest, contract lock digest, and canary suite version.
2. Verification & logging: unit tests assert any required-stage failure blocks, findings name stage + next action, and GateResult records gate/policy/contract-lock/canary digests on pass AND fail, emitting a structured per-stage GateResult + timestamped stage log; exercised by the vmr.14 harness and mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: agents can generate and judge, but the conductor owns the mechanical verdict.

title: Implement GateHealth freshness key and stale-canary fail-closed behavior
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Store GateHealth by project and freshness key over gate version, gate code digest, policy digest, test pack digest, container image digest, code-quality profile digest, canary suite version, and RunCheck schema version.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. any key change invalidates prior green canary; stale green result fails gate with stale_canary; latest canary output appears in artifacts and LiveView.
2. Verification & logging: tests assert any freshness-key change invalidates a prior green canary and a stale green result fails gate with stale_canary, emitting the freshness key + a structured staleness finding and surfacing the latest canary output; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: green canaries from yesterday's policy or test pack are not evidence for today's gate.

title: Create eval suites for plan audit, policy, prompt injection, artifacts, and adapters
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add labeled eval suites for plan_audit, policy, prompt_injection, artifact_integrity, adapter_conformance, and gate_canaries.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. suites produce stable pass/fail reports; prompt/tool-output injection cannot override locked contract or policy; tampered manifests/missing logs/mismatched hashes/stale canary refs fail RunCheck.
2. Verification & logging: the labeled eval suites (plan_audit, policy, prompt_injection, artifact_integrity, adapter_conformance, gate_canaries) produce stable pass/fail reports and assert injection cannot override locked contract/policy and tampered/missing/mismatched artifacts fail RunCheck, emitting a structured per-suite eval report; shared with vmr.8 and mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: canaries are the first eval suite, not a one-off subsystem. Keep the harness extensible but small.

title: Implement local stop-the-line behavior for critical gate/canary failures
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add local stop-the-line semantics for the sample project when critical gate/canary failures occur.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. merge is blocked; further sample project runs are blocked or clearly marked unsafe until addressed; operator output explains the blocking GateHealth/GateResult and next action.
2. Verification & logging: tests assert a critical gate/canary failure blocks merge and further sample runs until addressed, with operator output explaining the blocking GateHealth/GateResult + next action, emitting a structured stop-the-line incident record; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: future phases can pause conflict domains or swarms. Phase 1 only needs to prevent further trusted claims for the affected project.

title: Gate workspace integrity and diff-scope policy
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Validate base commit, PatchSet existence, clean application to fresh checkout, head tree digest, forbidden/protected files, locked contract/test/policy files, file count/line limits, dependency/migration/generated/public API changes, and DiffPolicy.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. unexpected scope changes fail or require human review according to policy; changed files and risk domains are recorded.
2. Verification & logging: fixtures for unexpected-scope/forbidden-path/locked-file/over-limit changes assert fail-or-human-review per policy with changed files + risk domains recorded, emitting a structured diff-scope finding + clean-apply log; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: never gate the dirty agent workspace. Always apply the recorded patch to a clean materialization.

title: Implement observed risk assessment and review policy escalation
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Classify observed risk from diff, command behavior, adapter capabilities, dependency changes, quality deltas, and touched paths through ReviewPolicy.risk_rules.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. risk above planned level deterministically escalates required reviews, requires human approval, or fails closed; rationale is recorded in RiskAssessment.
2. Verification & logging: fixtures assert risk above the planned level deterministically escalates reviews/human-approval/fail-closed with rationale recorded in RiskAssessment, emitting a structured risk-classification log; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: planned low risk is only a hypothesis. The diff may prove otherwise.

title: Gate test execution and acceptance mapping
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Re-run baseline regression and locked acceptance suites in clean gate workspace using structured result adapters.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. baseline stays green; locked acceptance calibrated red on base passes after patch; repeats/flake policy/infra retries are honored; every AC has passed evidence; missing/skipped evidence fails unless explicitly allowed.
2. Verification & logging: fixtures assert baseline stays green, calibrated-red acceptance passes after patch, flake/infra retries are honored, and missing/skipped AC evidence fails unless allowed, emitting per-suite structured results + an AC-to-evidence map on pass and fail; shared with vmr.11 and exercised by the vmr.14 harness and mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: implementation-time test output is advisory. Gate truth is the conductor's independent run.

title: Validate ContractLock and read-only locked TestPack at gate time
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Check that Brief, AC mapping, required tests, verification commands, AGENTS.md, policy profile, protected globs, and TestPack digest match the approved ContractLock.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. repository test changes may exist but cannot replace locked acceptance evidence; weakening/deleting required tests fails RunCheck/gate; mutable upstream changes create a new RunSpec.
2. Verification & logging: fixtures assert weakening/deleting required tests fails RunCheck/gate and mutable upstream changes force a new RunSpec, emitting a structured lock-comparison finding; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: this is where test weakening is caught even when the patch looks otherwise harmless.

title: Add quality-signal delta stage with advisory and blocking modes
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Integrate CodeQualityRun before/after results into the gate according to project policy.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Noop/local adapter works in default demo; configured blocking adapter must declare deterministic output/version/schema/fixture suite/threshold policy; new high-risk findings fail when threshold requires; quality output is not treated as sole proof.
2. Verification & logging: tests assert Noop/local works in the default demo, a configured blocking adapter must declare deterministic output/version/schema/fixture/threshold, and new high-risk findings fail when the threshold requires, emitting a structured before/after quality delta; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: CodeScent and similar tools are risk signals and context, not proof of correctness.

title: Implement reviewer-on-dossier prompt, schema, and actor separation
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add reviewer role that reads only recorded dossier/artifacts and returns schema-valid decision, recommendation, summary, findings, and checks.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. reviewer profile/session is distinct from implementer; malformed output fails; review stores dossier digest and rubric version; gate only uses reviews for the same dossier digest.

Verification & logging: unit tests create/read/update each resource through Ash and assert immutable fields are guarded; a migration test applies and rolls back cleanly; emits structured migration + guard-violation logs; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix (state-machine/ledger specifics covered by vmr.2).

comments.text: Future-self note: many tables are active because the tracer actually queries or gates them. Resist adding deferred resources until their lifecycle becomes executable.

title: Build Oban station topology and RunSlice orchestrator
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Implement Conveyor.Jobs.RunSlice plus station workers for readiness, baseline, acceptance calibration, context scout, implementer, evidence, reviewer, gate, canary, stale effect reconciliation, sandbox reaping, and artifact projection.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. the Phase 1 StationPlan executes linearly; each long-running station is durable; crash/reboot can resume from persisted StationRun state.
2. Verification & logging: an integration test drives the linear StationPlan and asserts crash/reboot resumes from persisted StationRun state; each station enqueue/dequeue emits a structured timeline entry + worker log on success and failure; exercised end-to-end by the vmr.14 harness and mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: one orchestrator does not mean one huge transaction. Keep station inputs/outputs digest-addressed so future parallel station DAGs fit without a rewrite.

title: Implement stale lease, unknown effect, and sandbox cleanup reconciliation
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add periodic workers that inspect expired leases, missing heartbeats, unknown StationEffects, Docker containers, workspace paths, credential leases, artifact blobs, and projections before deciding to resume, retry, cancel, fail, or cleanup.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. orphaned containers/workspaces are detected; cleanup status is recorded; failed cleanup creates actionable incidents/findings.
2. Verification & logging: tests cover expired-lease, missing-heartbeat, unknown-effect, orphan-container/workspace, and credential-lease fixtures and assert the resume/retry/cancel/cleanup decision with recorded status, emitting structured incident/cleanup findings; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: never assume the database is the whole truth after a crash. Reconciliation must inspect the external runtime before making claims.

title: Add database constraints for identity, idempotency, and immutability
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add constraints for unique requirement and decision stable keys, Slice position, AgentBrief/TestPack versions, RunSpec digest, RunAttempt attempt number, one active attempt per Slice in Phase 1, StationRun/StationEffect/LedgerEvent idempotency, Artifact digest identity, and GateHealth freshness key.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. duplicate fixture inserts fail at the DB boundary; immutable digest/base/lock/blob fields cannot be mutated in place; corrections require new rows and ledger events.
2. Verification & logging: tests assert duplicate stable-key/version/digest/one-active-attempt inserts fail at the DB boundary and immutable digest/base/lock/blob columns cannot be updated in place, emitting the constraint-violation error + structured finding; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: idempotency and evidence identity cannot live only in Ash validations. Crashes and retries need database-backed truth.

title: Implement immutable RunSpec and versioned StationPlan capsule
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Create RunSpec generation that freezes project/base commit, Slice, autonomy level, plan/decision/brief/ContractLock/AGENTS/policy/DiffPolicy/TestPack/verification/prompt/AgentProfile/toolchain/sandbox/budget/code-quality/canary/schema/station plan digests.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. RunSpec is content-addressed; every station input/output includes run_spec_sha256; contract-affecting changes create a new RunSpec and RunAttempt rather than mutating old evidence.
2. Verification & logging: unit tests assert RunSpec content-addressing is stable, every station input/output carries run_spec_sha256, and a contract-affecting change yields a NEW RunSpec instead of mutating evidence, emitting a structured digest-set summary + diff finding; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: RunSpec is the answer to what exactly did this run try to do. If that answer changes, the old evidence remains interpretable but cannot be silently reused.

title: Model Plan, Requirement, HumanDecision, Epic, Slice, and AgentBrief lifecycle
description:

title: Implement StationRun and StationEffect idempotency semantics
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Each station creates/resumes StationRun using run_attempt_id + station_key + station_spec_sha256 + attempt_no, declares external StationEffects before execution, writes outputs by digest, and can retry safely.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. retrying a completed station does not duplicate artifacts/events/transitions; changed inputs require a new attempt; unknown effects are reconciled before retry.
2. Verification & logging: unit tests assert retrying a completed station duplicates nothing, changed inputs force a new attempt, and unknown effects are reconciled before retry, emitting a structured idempotency-key + effect-state log; coverage shared with vmr.3 and mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: Oban uniqueness is useful but not sufficient. The domain idempotency key is the durable contract.

title: Phase 0: Foundations, product contract, scaffold, and doctor
description:
## Summary
Phase 0 foundation work establishes the deterministic Conveyor application skeleton and the operator-facing kernel required before any Slice is executable. It covers product contract docs, Phoenix/Ash/Oban/Postgres scaffold, runtime config, .conveyor initialization, doctor checks, and the first AGENTS.md generation path. The purpose is to make the project bootable, inspectable, and honest about prerequisites before higher-risk trust claims are layered on top.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Child tasks preserve the full feature and trust scope named in this epic; do not replace them with a narrower shortcut.
2. The Phase 0/1 verification matrix maps this epic and each child task to unit, integration, e2e or canary coverage plus required diagnostic logs/artifacts, or an explicit approved N/A.
3. Completion requires the relevant child tasks to satisfy their own acceptance criteria, dependency order, quality gates, and preserved run/evidence artifacts.

## Success Criteria
1. Relevant child tasks are complete with their own acceptance criteria, dependency order, quality gates, and evidence artifacts satisfied.
2. The Phase 0/1 verification matrix maps this epic and its child tasks to required unit, integration, e2e or canary coverage plus diagnostic logs/artifacts.
3. No feature, trust primitive, or user-visible workflow named in this epic is removed, narrowed, or silently deferred without an explicit human-approved contract change bead.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Rationale: this epic protects the public contract. Conveyor must first say exactly what it is allowed to do in Phase 1: L1 local implementation with L2-shaped artifacts, no auto-merge, no auto-deploy, and evidence as the product. Downstream epics depend on this because they need stable config, runtime assumptions, and operator commands.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Plan-space audit revision: this top-level epic is a planning container, not an implementation pick. Use its child tasks and dependency edges as the authoritative executable graph; the epic priority is intentionally lower so br/bv triage surfaces concrete work first.

title: Write Phase 0/1 product contract and autonomy docs
description:

Create or update VISION.md, AUTONOMY_LEVELS.md, SAFETY_POLICY.md, TASK_SCHEMA.md, EVIDENCE_SCHEMA.md, and ARCHITECTURE.md so the project has a self-contained statement of purpose, non-goals, autonomy levels L0-L4, the Phase 1 L1 target, evidence requirements, trust boundaries, and explicit deferrals.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. docs consistently state that Phase 1 produces PR-quality evidence but does not auto-merge or deploy; docs identify Conveyor as deterministic conductor plus stochastic agents; docs name the factory-kernel primitives that must not be cut.
2. Verification & logging: a docs lint/CI check asserts VISION/AUTONOMY_LEVELS/SAFETY_POLICY/TASK_SCHEMA/EVIDENCE_SCHEMA/ARCHITECTURE exist with required sections and the no-auto-merge + L1-target statements, emitting structured findings naming any missing section; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: these docs are not marketing copy. They are the contract that keeps scope from drifting into a generic agent platform, issue tracker, LLM framework, or deployment system.

title: Capture ADRs for Phase 0/1 trust-boundary decisions
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Write short ADRs for the decisions that future implementers must not accidentally reverse: BEAM/Ash conductor owns truth, evidence is product artifact, Docker is not the security boundary, locked TestPack and ContractLock are never-cut primitives, gate canaries fail closed, Phase 1 is L1 with L2-shaped artifacts, and merge/deploy authority is deferred.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. each ADR states context, decision, consequences, rejected alternatives, and which beads depend on the decision.
2. Verification & logging: a docs lint asserts each ADR states context/decision/consequences/rejected-alternatives/dependent-beads, emitting structured findings for incomplete ADRs; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.
close_reason: Completed ADRs and ADR contract lint

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: ADRs preserve the reasoning behind the plan, not just the tasks. They make later refactors safer because future agents can see why the seams exist.

title: Define local developer bootstrap and troubleshooting path
description:
## Summary

Define the local developer setup path for Conveyor itself: required system packages, Postgres setup, Docker expectations, Mix setup command, environment variables, sample repo location, common doctor failures, and how to reset only generated demo state without deleting user work.

## Acceptance Criteria

2. Verification & logging: the documented clone->doctor->demo path is exercised by the vmr.14 e2e harness (or a smoke) asserting destructive cleanup is excluded/approval-gated, capturing the bootstrap transcript + structured step summary; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

comments.text: Future-self note: doctor is useful only if users know how to satisfy it. Keep setup instructions boring and exact.

title: Scaffold Phoenix, Ash, Oban, and Postgres application
description:
## Summary

## Acceptance Criteria
1. application boots locally; Repo starts; Oban is configured; LiveView endpoint starts; mix test and mix format --check-formatted can run in CI; exact runtime/library versions are discoverable for RunSpec recording.
2. Verification & logging: an integration smoke boots the app, starts Repo/Oban/LiveView endpoint, and runs mix test + mix format --check-formatted; the boot/version probe writes structured JSON of resolved Elixir/OTP/library/Postgres versions (for RunSpec) plus a captured boot log on success and failure; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix and runnable in baseline CI (hsh.7).
close_reason: Completed Phoenix/Ash/Oban/Postgres scaffold with version probe, Oban migration, smoke tests, runtime docs, and verified Postgres-backed checks.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: keep the scaffold boring. The goal is a durable BEAM control plane, not a custom framework or premature distributed scheduler.

comments.author: BluePond
comments.text: BluePond verification pass: ran hsh.2 scaffold checks in Docker Elixir 1.17/OTP 27 as UID 1000 with isolated MIX_DEPS_PATH/MIX_BUILD_ROOT under container /tmp. Artifact log: tmp/bluepond_hsh2_mix_20260616T200440Z.log. deps resolved; Conveyor compiled 13 files; mix format --check-formatted completed before tests. mix test failed at application start because local Postgres requires SCRAM auth and POSTGRES_PASSWORD was not supplied/known, causing Repo/Oban startup failure. This looks like an environment credential blocker, not a compile/format failure.

title: Implement .conveyor config model and runtime loader
description:
## Summary

## Acceptance Criteria

2. Verification & logging: unit tests load a valid config and reject malformed/missing-command/duplicate-profile fixtures with actionable findings, and assert project-local config cannot override a locked RunSpec mid-run; the loader emits a structured JSON of resolved profiles/commands plus redaction-safe parse findings; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.
close_reason: Completed .conveyor config model and runtime loader with targeted verification

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Future-self note: project config is a mutable source before RunSpec creation and an immutable digest after. That boundary prevents accidental evidence drift.

comments.author: MossyShore
comments.text: Completed .conveyor config loader verification. Implemented/verified deterministic ProjectConfig resolution with run_spec_lock_behavior in the digest, standard policy profile coverage, command specs for PlanAudit/AGENTS.md/policy/verification, locked RunSpec digest/override rejection, and config_probe JSON emission. Verification: docker elixir:1.17-slim mix format --check-formatted on touched Elixir files passed; docker mix test --no-start test/conveyor/project_config_test.exs passed (5 tests, 0 failures); docker mix conveyor.config_probe emitted conveyor.config.resolution@1 status=ok with 4 commands and 0 findings; git diff --check passed; bash -n scripts/ci_control_plane.sh passed; py_compile and docs/check scripts passed; ubs on changed files passed. Full mix test without --no-start still requires reachable Postgres/Oban and was not used for this pure loader gate.

title: Implement actionable doctor checks for runtime prerequisites

title: Implement ProjectArtifacts/report station for run bundle projection

2. Each row names expected unit/integration/e2e-or-canary coverage, fixture names, commands, artifact/log paths, and blocking vs advisory behavior.
3. Any missing test layer requires explicit approved N/A rationale; TRUST_REQUIRED and TRACER_REQUIRED work cannot silently omit verification.
4. Required diagnostic evidence includes command transcript, structured JSON summary, run bundle digest, failure category, NextAction, redaction status, and schema/version refs where applicable.
5. CI or plan-audit enforcement fails when a required bead lacks mapped verification or when mapped commands are stale.
6. The matrix explicitly covers every user-visible operator surface (mix conveyor.init, doctor, plan_audit, seed_sample, demo, show, run_slice, verify, gate_canary, report, replay, contract_diff, ci) and the LiveView timeline/controls, each mapped to integration or e2e coverage with named log/artifact paths.
7. Every TRACER_REQUIRED/TRUST_REQUIRED implementation bead carries its own mapped unit/integration coverage AND a detailed-logging obligation (command transcript + structured JSON summary + redaction-safe station/command logs on success and failure); each matrix row links to the bead's own Verification & logging acceptance criterion so the obligation is enforceable even before this matrix bead is implemented.
notes: Purpose: make the whole Phase 0/1 plan auditable before implementation without dropping any feature scope.

title: Build logged e2e script harness for hermetic Phase-1 tracer
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED. Add e2e script entrypoints, including scripts/e2e_test.sh unless a project-standard name supersedes it, that run the deterministic demo, mix conveyor.ci, gate canaries, artifact replay/report checks, and failure-fixture modes without live credentials, CodeScent, or network egress. The harness must be usable locally and in default CI and must preserve detailed logs for every station and command.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. scripts/e2e_test.sh or the chosen project-standard e2e entrypoint runs hermetic known-good demo, mix conveyor.ci, gate canaries, report replay, and representative failure fixtures.
2. Script uses strict shell behavior, timestamped step banners, command transcript capture, environment summary, deterministic output directory, and final artifact index.
3. Produces structured JSON and JUnit where appropriate plus raw logs for demo, CI command, gate canaries, RunCheck, report replay, and exit-code checks.
4. Covers known-good plus representative failures for Conveyor exit classes 2-7, with stable categories and redacted secrets.
5. Default mode has no live provider credentials, no CodeScent requirement, and no network egress; live Pi/provider tests remain tagged/manual.
6. CI preserves the e2e log directory and run bundle on both success and failure.
7. The harness also exercises the contributor onboarding surface hermetically: a mix conveyor.doctor smoke and a mix conveyor.init smoke (in a throwaway directory), mix conveyor.plan_audit over a good and a blocked plan fixture, and mix conveyor.contract_diff over a weakened-contract fixture, each capturing transcript, structured JSON, and asserting its stable exit code.
8. Every station (readiness, baseline, acceptance calibration, scout, prompt, implement, evidence, review, gate, canary freshness, report) writes its own timestamped per-station log section plus a machine-readable per-station status line (station_key, status, duration, finding category) so a reader can confirm each station ran and exactly how it ended.
9. A final machine-readable run index aggregates per-station status, overall exit code, run bundle root digest, and exact log/artifact paths for both success and failure runs, so a failure can be reproduced locally from the printed paths alone.

title: Add LiveView integration tests for timeline and operator controls
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Add Phoenix LiveViewTest coverage for the operator LiveView: project/plan/epic/slice hierarchy, PlanAudit score/findings, Slice and RunAttempt state, the StationRun/LedgerEvent timeline updating live from the outbox/PubSub path, and the evidence/gate/canary/manual-integration panels and export controls.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. a seeded Slice renders and the timeline updates live as LedgerEvents append; no artifact truth exists only in LiveView (every rendered value traces to a DB record or projected artifact).
2. evidence AC mapping, quality delta, reviewer verdict, gate stages, canary status, and incidents render from recorded artifacts; the manual external-integration control requires an external commit hash or an explicit not_integrated decision.
3. tests assert against rendered markup and pushed updates deterministically without live providers; a failure identifies which panel or event contract regressed.
4. Verification & logging: the LiveView test run emits a structured JSON summary plus captured rendered-state snapshots on failure; coverage is mapped in the vmr.13 verification matrix.

title: Test state machines and ledger event emission
description:

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Plan/Slice/RunAttempt states match design; blocked transitions explain guard failure; duplicate idempotency keys are rejected.
2. Logging & evidence: the suite emits a structured per-case JSON summary plus deterministic failure findings (category + NextAction) and preserves the raw log/artifact for each failing case so CI shows exactly which transition/event regressed; registered in the vmr.13 matrix.

title: Trust kernel: safety policy, sandbox, command grammar, and credentials
description:
## Summary
Implement explicit safety controls around every executable station: Policy profiles, normalized command grammar, denylist/allowlist evaluation, environment and network policy, Docker sandbox defaults, CredentialLease handling, incidents, budget stops, and command recording. Docker is necessary but not sufficient; policy defines intent.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Child tasks preserve the full feature and trust scope named in this epic; do not replace them with a narrower shortcut.
2. The Phase 0/1 verification matrix maps this epic and each child task to unit, integration, e2e or canary coverage plus required diagnostic logs/artifacts, or an explicit approved N/A.
3. Completion requires the relevant child tasks to satisfy their own acceptance criteria, dependency order, quality gates, and preserved run/evidence artifacts.

## Success Criteria
1. Relevant child tasks are complete with their own acceptance criteria, dependency order, quality gates, and evidence artifacts satisfied.
2. The Phase 0/1 verification matrix maps this epic and its child tasks to required unit, integration, e2e or canary coverage plus diagnostic logs/artifacts.
3. No feature, trust primitive, or user-visible workflow named in this epic is removed, narrowed, or silently deferred without an explicit human-approved contract change bead.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Rationale: the agent adapter is not the security boundary. Conveyor must enforce policy through sandbox constraints and ToolExecutor-mediated commands, and it must record violations as evidence. This epic gates any credible L1 run and prevents later autonomy from growing on an unsafe base.

1. allowed sample commands run; forbidden commands are blocked pre-exec when adapter capabilities permit; observe-only adapter modes are marked and capped.

Verification & logging: integration tests assert allowed sample commands run, forbidden commands are blocked pre-exec where capabilities permit, and observe-only modes are marked/capped; every attempt writes a ToolInvocation with command spec/profile/decision/timing/output-digest and a structured per-command transcript on success and failure; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

title: Implement Docker sandbox defaults and capability checks
description:
## Summary
Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED. Define Docker sandbox defaults: non-root user, rootless preferred, no privileged containers, no Docker socket, no host home mount, read-only mounts for contracts/policies/.conveyor, read-write workspace only, no-new-privileges, seccomp/AppArmor where available, resource/output/time limits, and network none by default.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. doctor reports host capabilities; selected policy profile fails if required sandbox constraints are unavailable.
2. Verification & logging: tests assert non-root/no-privileged/no-socket/no-home-mount/read-only-contract-mounts/network-none defaults and that doctor fails the profile when a required constraint is unavailable, emitting a structured host-capability + applied-constraint report; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.

title: Implement station-level network and egress policy

## Acceptance Criteria

2. Verification & logging: tests assert the sandbox cannot reach Conveyor Postgres/ledger/internal services, approved egress is external-host-only, and unapproved attempts are recorded/blocked, emitting a structured network-decision log + violation finding; mapped in the vmr.13 matrix.
